assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as RPM based on the library name (openssl-libs) and component context, not Cargo (Step 1 Ecosystem detection — read ecosystems from Ecosystem Mappings config)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md lines 31-32: 'The vulnerable package **openssl-libs** is a system RPM package. Ecosystem: **RPM**.' The ecosystem is identified as RPM based on the library name openssl-libs. Cargo is never mentioned. The Ecosystem Mappings config table is referenced on lines 36-39 showing RPM ecosystem with rpms.lock.yaml lock file.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the 2.2.x stream supportability matrix (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) and uses rpms.lock.yaml data for version extraction (Step 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 7-13 contain all five versions: 2.2.0 (v0.4.5, 3.0.7-25.el9_3), 2.2.1 (v0.4.8, 3.0.7-27.el9_4), 2.2.2 (v0.4.9, retag of 2.2.1), 2.2.3 (v0.4.11, 3.0.7-28.el9_4), 2.2.4 (v0.4.12, 3.0.7-28.el9_4). The dependency chain section on line 29 states 'rpms.lock.yaml: present --> explicit install' confirming rpms.lock.yaml is used for version extraction.

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context identifies openssl-libs as present in rpms.lock.yaml and uses this to determine the package origin — the lock file presence is the primary classification signal (Step 2.3.5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 28-36: 'Dependency chain for openssl-libs (RPM): rpms.lock.yaml: present --> explicit install ... Origin: explicit install (openssl-libs specified in rpms.lock.yaml)'. Lines 38-41 further confirm: 'Since openssl-libs is found in rpms.lock.yaml at each inspected tag, it is classified as an **explicit install** -- not inherited from a base image.' SBOM verification was skipped, making lock file presence the sole/primary classification signal (line 31: 'Using rpms.lock.yaml classification only').

assertion_results.text: Remediation creates a single task for the Konflux release repo — not the two-task upstream backport + downstream propagation flow used for source dependency ecosystems (Step 8 Remediation Task Creation — system package path)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 12-14: 'Since openssl-libs is an RPM system package with an explicit install origin (present in rpms.lock.yaml), a single remediation task is created in the Konflux release repo. No upstream backport task is needed for RPM packages.' Only one jira.create_issue call is present (lines 20-27). The task targets the Konflux release repo 'rhtpa-release.0.4.z' (line 35). No second upstream backport task is created.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task description follows task-description-template.md format with Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections — Files to Modify is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md (Step 8 Remediation Task Creation)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md task description contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 33, value: rhtpa-release.0.4.z), '## Target Branch' (line 37, value: main), '## Description' (line 41), '## Implementation Notes' (line 64), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 79). No '## Files to Modify' section exists anywhere in the task description, confirming intentional omission per remediation-templates.md.

assertion_results.text: The version impact output addresses SBOM verification in the dependency chain context — either noting that verification was skipped because cosign is not available or because external tools are prohibited, or presenting the SBOM classification result (Step 2.3.5 Optional SBOM verification)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 30-31: 'SBOM verification: skipped -- cosign not available in this environment. Using rpms.lock.yaml classification only.' Lines 43-45 reiterate: 'SBOM verification via cosign was not performed because cosign is not available in this environment. The rpms.lock.yaml classification stands as the sole source of origin determination.' This explicitly addresses SBOM verification and provides the reason it was skipped.
